content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (Jun 9, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on June 9 focused on optimizing Eliza Cloud infrastructure and app deployment. Key achievements include implementing cost-effective, optional tenant-DB nodes, hardening multi-project Hetzner deployments, and finalizing the app-ingress and database teardown logic to ensure a stable go-live for Product 2.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Cloud Infrastructure & Cost Optimization**
*   Made dedicated tenant-DB nodes optional to reduce fixed costs for low-density apps ([#8344](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8344)).
*   Implemented a multi-project Hetzner pattern to bypass server quota limits ([#8354](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8354)).
*   Hardened Terraform pre-apply workflows with fail-closed variables and state locking ([#8359](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8359)).
*   Added GHA workflows for control-plane management ([#8358](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8358)) and fixed retired server type defaults ([#8337](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8337), [#8360](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8360)).

**App Deployment & Database Lifecycle**
*   Enabled opt-in per-tenant databases and automated teardown/slot-release upon app deletion ([#8338](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8338), [#8343](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8343)).
*   Integrated verified custom domains into the apps ingress stack ([#8335](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8335)).
*   Improved autoscaler efficiency by adding environment-overridable buffer settings and proper tier labeling ([#8353](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8353), [#8348](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8348)).

**Tooling & Maintenance**
*   Repaired the cloud-sdk route-coverage audit tool ([#8334](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8334)).
*   Added drift protection for apps-data-plane VMs by ignoring name changes ([#8349](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8349)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#8356](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8356): Infrastructure fixes for pgbouncer, ports, and env contracts.
*   [#8355](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8355) & [#8350](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8350): Steward OAuth PKCE implementation for Feed and login surfaces.
*   [#8352](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8352): Gate npm publish on global-install smoke tests.
*   [#8351](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8351): Billing, ingress, and tenant DB teardown fixes.
*   [#8347](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8347) & [#8346](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8346): Registry updates for third-party plugins (Reddit/Neynar).

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   None.

## NEW ISSUES
*   [#8345](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8345): Runtime Open Federation — Proposal for a permissionless, multi-agent network allowing agents to earn USDC rewards.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   [#8257](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8257): **Steward Login Issues.** Email login is confirmed fixed. Google OAuth remains blocked due to a missing tenant-level redirect allowlist. The team has identified the specific tenant-config action required to resolve this.
*   [#8321](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8321): **Eliza Cloud Apps Scaling.** The team has completed the code-side requirements (ingress, isolation, DB teardown). The focus has shifted to final infrastructure hardening and coordinating the daemon restart for the new staging environment.
